behaviors.description: Command segmentation treats newline-separated statements as separate segments, not one monolithic block.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the segmentation rule itself (single parser/normalizer logic). No module handoff, filesystem, subprocess, network, or model output is required. Rejected Integration because the behavior text only asserts segmentation correctness, not cross-module state wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Call the segmentation function with a multi-line shell snippet (assignments + command) and assert it returns distinct segments per statement/newline boundary instead of one combined segment.

behaviors.description: A multi-line shell block with variable assignments followed by `gh pr create` is recognized as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: Incident failure was classifier output (`not_a_trigger`) caused by segmented-input handling, so minimum boundary is parser/segmenter + trigger-classifier wiring. Rejected System: no real subprocess/network/OS contract needed to detect trigger text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI decision path.
behaviors.test_description: Run the PostToolUse classification pipeline end-to-end in-process on a multi-line command block (`VAR=...` lines then `gh pr create ...`) and assert trigger type is PR-create.

behaviors.description: A similar multi-line block that ends with `gh issue create` is not incorrectly treated as a PR-create trigger.
behaviors.justification: This is negative classification at the same module boundary as behavior 2: segmented input must be handed correctly into trigger detection with command-type specificity. Rejected System because no real `gh` execution/external API behavior is part of correctness text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the same in-process PostToolUse parsing+classification flow for a multi-line block ending in `gh issue create` and assert result is not PR-create (e.g., `not_a_trigger` or issue-specific class).

behaviors.description: When a PR-create trigger is detected, the hook can reconstruct and persist the PR identifier needed by the review loop state.
behaviors.justification: Requires local handoff between trigger-detection output, PR-id reconstruction logic, and persistence adapter/file format. Rejected Unit because persistence and cross-module handoff are intrinsic to behavior. Rejected System despite file I/O consideration: behavior is satisfiable with local in-process temp filesystem; no OS-specific primitives/subprocess/network required.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke PostToolUse handler with a detected PR-create event fixture, then assert persisted state contains the reconstructed PR identifier in expected field/path using a temp workspace/state directory.

behaviors.description: After PR-create detection, the review gate state is written in a form that the pre-tool enforcement hook can read and enforce.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is cross-module contract: writer (PostToolUse) and reader/enforcer (PreToolUse) must agree on persisted schema/state semantics. Rejected System because this can be validated in one process with local test filesystem and both modules wired together; no external contracts needed. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: In one integration test, run PostToolUse state-write path for PR-create, then run PreToolUse enforcement read path against the same state artifact and assert enforcement activates (not bypassed) for gated commands.
